name: SpyCloudSCORCH
displayName: SCORCH — SpyCloud Identity Exposure Intelligence Agent
description: AI-powered Security Copilot agent that investigates dark web credential exposures, infostealer infections, and identity threats using SpyCloud intelligence integrated with Microsoft Sentinel.

skills.name: InvestigateExposure
skills.description: Deep investigation of a compromised identity including breach context, credential analysis, sign-in correlation, device forensics, and blast radius assessment.

inputs.name: email
inputs.description: Email address or UPN of the exposed identity

inputs.name: depth
inputs.description: Investigation depth: quick (summary only), standard (full analysis), deep (includes threat research)

skills.name: GenerateExecutiveReport
skills.description: Create a board-ready executive summary of the organization's dark web exposure posture with risk metrics, trend analysis, and strategic recommendations.

inputs.name: domain
inputs.description: Organization domain to generate report for (e.g., company.com)

skills.name: ThreatResearch
skills.description: Research threat actors, malware families, APT groups, and attack techniques across dark web forums, blogs, social media, whitepapers, and threat intelligence platforms.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: Threat research query — can be a malware family, APT group, CVE, IOC, or general threat topic

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Additional context such as incident ID, affected systems, or timeframe

skills.name: IncidentReport
skills.description: Create a detailed incident report for a Sentinel incident including timeline, affected entities, MITRE ATT&CK mapping, and remediation steps.

skills.name: RemediationPlan
skills.description: Create a prioritized remediation plan with step-by-step actions for password resets, session revocation, device isolation, Conditional Access updates, and user notification.

inputs.name: email
inputs.description: Email address of the exposed user to generate remediation plan for

inputs.name: includePrevention
inputs.description: Whether to include long-term prevention recommendations

skills.name: QuerySpyCloudData
skills.description: Execute KQL queries against SpyCloud custom log tables in Sentinel. Supports all 14 SpyCloud tables including breach watchlist, compass data, SIP cookies, and IDLink.

inputs.name: query
inputs.description: KQL query to execute against SpyCloud tables

skills.name: BlastRadiusAnalysis
skills.description: Analyze the blast radius of a compromised entity using Sentinel Graph to map all reachable assets, lateral movement paths, and exposure perimeter.

inputs.name: entity
inputs.description: Entity identifier (email, hostname, or IP address)

body.context: Perform blast radius analysis for this {entity_type} entity. Use Sentinel Graph to map all reachable assets, identify lateral movement paths, and calculate the exposure perimeter.

promptExamples: Investigate the exposure for user john.doe@contoso.com
Generate an executive report for the last 30 days
Research the RedLine Stealer malware family
What is the blast radius if admin@contoso.com is compromised?
Create a remediation plan for Sentinel incident INC-2026-0412
Show me all severity 25 exposures from the last week
How many users have plaintext passwords exposed on the dark web?
What are the top malware families targeting our organization?
systemPrompt: You are SCORCH (SpyCloud Orchestrated Response & Cyber Hunt), an expert identity threat intelligence analyst. You specialize in investigating dark web credential exposures, infostealer infections, and identity-based attacks. You have access to SpyCloud's recaptured darknet data integrated with Microsoft Sentinel, including breach watchlists, infostealer telemetry, session cookies, and identity correlation data across 14 custom log tables. You provide actionable analysis with MITRE ATT&CK mapping, severity-based prioritization (2=breach credential, 5=breach+PII, 20=infostealer credential, 25=infostealer+app data), and concrete remediation steps. You are thorough, precise, and security-focused.

copilotStudioIntegration.description: This agent can be published to Microsoft Copilot Studio for broader distribution. Follow these steps to integrate:
steps: 2. Create a new custom copilot or extend an existing one
3. Add this agent as a plugin via 'Add a plugin' > 'Custom connector'
4. Configure the AI Engine URL endpoint as the base URL
5. Map each skill to a Copilot Studio topic
6. Configure authentication using Managed Identity or API key
7. Test the integration using the built-in test panel
8. Publish to your organization's channels (Teams, Slack, Web)

defenderPortalIntegration.description: To make this agent available in the Microsoft Defender portal:
steps: 1. Ensure Security Copilot is enabled in your tenant
2. Go to Security Copilot > Settings > Custom plugins
4. Configure the AI Engine URL in the plugin settings
5. Assign appropriate RBAC roles to the agent
6. The agent will appear in the Copilot sidebar within Defender portal
